steps.title: Use nearby trend values
steps.content: The scatterplot shows an increasing linear trend. At $60\%$ concentration, the predicted density should be between the trend values near $53\%$ and $67\%$.

steps.title: Estimate the middle value
steps.content:
The nearby densities are about $1{,}255$ and $1{,}340$. Since $60\%$ is roughly between the two concentrations, estimate a density near the middle.
$$\frac{1{,}255+1{,}340}{2}=1{,}297.5$$

steps.title: Choose the closest value
steps.content: The closest listed value to this estimate is $1{,}300$. The answer is C.
